claim: Documents serve as the foundation layer for agentic systems - they are the primary input and source of truth for real-world AI decision-making in regulated industries
actionable: Design agentic workflows to start with document extraction and parsing as the foundational layer before any reasoning or decision-making occurs
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | David Park: Building Production Grade Agentic Systems with ADE

claim: Deterministic shells around stochastic cores - wrap LLM outputs with validation, typed interfaces, field validation, and business logic to constrain model behavior in production systems
actionable: Implement typed input/output schemas, validation layers, and explicit business rules around each LLM call rather than relying solely on model reasoning
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | David Park: Building Production Grade Agentic Systems with ADE

claim: Separate data ingestion from agentic reasoning with caching - extract and structure documents once, then cache results to avoid repeated LLM calls on identical data
actionable: Implement a two-phase system: Phase 1 parses/extracts/caches documents; Phase 2 runs reasoning and scenarios on cached data without re-invoking extraction models
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | David Park: Building Production Grade Agentic Systems with ADE

claim: Visual grounding is critical for audit trails - highlight exactly where extracted information originated in source documents to enable compliance and traceability
actionable: When extracting from documents, implement visual grounding that traces every value back to pixel-level source data for regulatory compliance
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | David Park: Building Production Grade Agentic Systems with ADE

claim: Use a hierarchical agent structure with one manager agent that owns the final decision, with specialized agents below producing evidence
actionable: Design agent systems with a clear decision hierarchy: one manager agent approves/denies/escalates, while specialized agents below produce evidence and reasoning
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | David Park: Building Production Grade Agentic Systems with ADE

claim: Schemas before prompts - start with type contracts and input/output schemas rather than crafting prompts first, enabling easier testing and reasoning about system behavior
actionable: Define clear input/output schemas for each agent before writing prompts; this enables schema validation and reduces debugging of convoluted reasoning chains
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | David Park: Building Production Grade Agentic Systems with ADE

claim: Use Google ADK (or LangChain/AWS Trainium) for orchestration to manage structured agent execution and context flow between agents
actionable: Leverage orchestration frameworks like Google ADK, LangChain, or AWS Trainium to handle context management and agent coordination rather than building from scratch
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | David Park: Building Production Grade Agentic Systems with ADE

claim: Claude (especially Haiku) is effective for reasoning and explanation layers in document-focused agentic systems while keeping costs down
actionable: Use Claude Haiku for reasoning, decision explanation, and natural language interaction in production agentic systems to balance cost and capability
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | David Park: Building Production Grade Agentic Systems with ADE

claim: In production systems, explicit business rules and deterministic logic in code are preferable to relying on LLM-generated reasoning for justifying decisions
actionable: Encode domain expertise as explicit Python rules that override or validate LLM outputs; this makes decisions defensible to risk teams and auditors
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | David Park: Building Production Grade Agentic Systems with ADE

claim: Implement human-in-the-loop for edge cases - agents should be able to escalate borderline decisions, missing data, or low-confidence outputs to humans for review
actionable: Configure manager agents to escalate low-confidence, missing-data, or borderline cases to human review rather than forcing an automated decision
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | David Park: Building Production Grade Agentic Systems with ADE

claim: Achieved 60% reduction in manual review time for a tier-1 global bank with 1.5 trillion dollars in assets by using agentic document extraction
actionable: Deploy similar agentic document extraction architectures for document-heavy processes (KYC, contract review, claims processing) to achieve comparable time savings
source_title: AI Dev 26 x SF | David Park: Building Production Grade Agentic Systems with ADE
